query:
scoping 445... your thoughts? ---                                                                                                                                                              
  Concrete design                                                 
                 
  Config (backward-compat, no migration):
  [knowledge]                                                                                                                                                      
  categories = ["lesson-learned", "decision", "convention", "pattern", "procedure"]
  adaptive_categories = ["lesson-learned"]   # default — everything else is pinned                                                                                 
                                                                                                                                                                   
  adaptive_categories defaults to ["lesson-learned"]. Existing configs that omit it get the correct default behavior. No format break.
                                                                                                                                                                   
  Runtime: Extend CategoryAllowlist to carry lifecycle — add a second HashSet<String> for adaptive categories and an is_adaptive(&self, category: &str) -> bool    
  method. Startup validation: every entry in adaptive_categories must also be in categories (fail fast if not).                                                    
                                                                                                                                                                   
  context_status: Add a category policy section to the output — each category listed with its lifecycle. Gives operators visibility without digging into config.   
   
  Maintenance tick: Add a lifecycle guard stub — if !category_policy.is_adaptive(entry.category) { continue; } before any future retention processing. The guard is
   the hook; #409 fills in the body.                              
                                                                                                                                                                   
  ---                                                             
  What this leaves open (intentionally)
                                                                                                                                                                   
  - What "adaptive" actually does to entries (decay rate, deprecation threshold, scoring weight) — all deferred to #409 and beyond
  - Whether adaptive affects PPR personalization weighting — deferred                                                                                              
  - K-parameter retention alignment with #409's query_log/co_access cleanup — deferred

entries.title: ADR-001 crt-031: CategoryAllowlist lifecycle policy constructor, config model, and StatusService wiring
Category retirement in INITIAL_CATEGORIES requires updates in 4 independent locations
KnowledgeConfig parallel list fields follow the boosted_categories structural pattern
KnowledgeConfig parallel list defaults collide in validate_config test fixtures — zero ALL parallel lists together
ADR-004: Separate /store-pattern Skill with What/Why/Scope Template
